description: Full-preset hot-swap defers to the MASTER loop boundary, not a rhythm's own loop. A1_master has mstrLoop=4 (64-step / 8.0s master loop @120bpm) and rhythm 0 looping every 4 steps (0.5s). A swap to the empty TS2 is requested at 1.0s; a correct commit waits until the 8.0s master boundary. The 'Well this is nice' bug committed at rhythm 0's loop (~1.5s) instead. Render is 9s so both the long pre-commit hold and the post-commit silence are observable.

assertions.name: plays_until_master_boundary
assertions.comment: kicks must still be sounding at 6.5-7.9s — proves the swap did NOT commit early at rhythm 0's 0.5s loop (the bug); the master boundary is 8.0s

assertions.name: swapped_after_master_boundary
assertions.comment: by 8.6s the swap to empty TS2 has committed at the 8.0s master boundary and TS1's tail has decayed — output is silent
